notes: MISMATCH: Satya states Microsoft Cloud revenue 'exceeded $54 billion'. The MDA files it precisely as $54.5 billion ($54,500M). 'Exceeded $54 billion' implies the figure is above $54B but states no upper bound — when compared to the filed $54,500M, the stated value understates by $500M. This is a deliberate understatement for emphasis ('exceeded'), not a rounding error. A system treating 'exceeded $54 billion' as equivalent to the filed $54.5B is making an inference the exec did not make; a precise verification must flag that the stated lower bound ($54B) does not equal the filed value ($54.5B). Verdict: mismatch on the stated figure — the exec understated the precise number.

payload.verbal_claim: Microsoft Cloud, which exceeded $54 billion in revenue, up 29% year-over-year

source.detail: MDA: 'Microsoft Cloud revenue increased 29% to $54.5 billion.' MS Cloud is a cross-segment metric (M365 Commercial Cloud + Azure + LinkedIn commercial + Dynamics 365).

ground_truth.tolerance_rule: exact; 'exceeded $54B' is a floor statement, not a rounded figure. The filed value is $54,500M = $54.5B. A system accepting 'exceeded $54B' as equivalent to $54.5B is inferring beyond what was stated.

locator.speaker: Satya Nadella
locator.quote_span: Microsoft Cloud, which exceeded $54 billion in revenue, up 29% year-over-year

notes: Near-duplicate boilerplate stressor: the phrase 'continued investments in AI infrastructure and growing AI product usage, offset in part by efficiency gains across the Microsoft Cloud' appears multiple times in the MSFT MDA — once in the total company GM% discussion, once in the MS Cloud GM% discussion, once in the segment gross margin sections. A retrieval system must surface the correct occurrence (total company GM%) not a near-identical adjacent chunk. BM25 will likely return all occurrences; ranking them correctly is the stressor.

payload.query: what drove the decrease in Microsoft total company gross margin percentage in Q3 FY2026
payload.expected_answer_gist: Total company gross margin percentage decreased driven by continued investments in AI infrastructure and growing AI product usage, offset in part by efficiency gains in Azure and M365 Commercial Cloud

ground_truth.distractor_note: The exact phrase 'continued investments in AI infrastructure and growing AI product usage, offset in part by efficiency gains' appears at least 3 times in the MDA — for total company GM%, for Microsoft Cloud GM%, and within segment discussions. A retrieval system returning the wrong occurrence (e.g. the MS Cloud GM% paragraph instead of total company) would answer a related but different question. The correct anchor is the total company GM% discussion in the Three Months summary section.

notes: False positive / formatting artifact: MSFT MDA uses a bullet-point structure that varies slightly between quarters in spacing, ordering of bullet items, and section headers. The underlying content of the 'Revenue drivers' bullets is substantively the same; only presentation formatting differs. A diff-based comparison tool will flag this as a change; it is not substantive.

payload.current_quarter_lang: • Gross margin percentage decreased driven by continued investments in AI infrastructure and growing AI product usage, offset in part by efficiency gains across the Microsoft Cloud. • Microsoft Cloud gross margin percentage decreased to 66%
payload.prior_quarter_lang: • Gross margin percentage decreased driven by continued investments in AI infrastructure and growing AI product usage, offset in part by efficiency gains across the Microsoft Cloud. • Microsoft Cloud gross margin percentage decreased to 69%
payload.why_not: The structural template and causal attribution language is identical. Only the percentage figure (66% vs 69%) changed, which reflects the underlying numeric update, not a language shift in characterization. A system flagging this as a 'language shift' is conflating a numeric update with a qualitative change.

locator.paragraph_hint: Microsoft Cloud gross margin percentage decreased to 66%
Microsoft Cloud gross margin percentage decreased to 69%

notes: False positive / reordering: MSFT's segment revenue summary bullets consistently list Intelligent Cloud before More Personal Computing. Within the MPC discussion, sub-items (Windows, Search, Gaming, Devices) appear in varying order across quarters without substantive meaning. A system detecting bullet reorder as a shift would over-flag.

payload.current_quarter_lang: Windows OEM and Devices revenue decreased 2%. Xbox content and services revenue decreased 5%. Search advertising revenue excluding traffic acquisition costs increased 12%.
payload.prior_quarter_lang: Search advertising revenue excluding traffic acquisition costs increased 21%. Windows OEM and Devices revenue increased 4%. Xbox content and services revenue decreased 4%.
payload.why_not: The sub-items are the same three metrics (Windows, Xbox, Search) in different order with updated figures. The order reflects no strategic signal — it varies with the emphasis of that quarter's discussion. The numeric figures changed (reflecting actual business results) but the qualitative framework is unchanged. This is a reordering of standard disclosure items, not a shift in characterization.

locator.paragraph_hint: Windows OEM and Devices revenue decreased 2% Xbox content and services
Search advertising revenue excluding traffic acquisition costs increased 21%

notes: Unanswerable: the $37B AI ARR figure Satya and Amy both cite is a non-GAAP KPI disclosed only on the earnings call — it does not appear in the 10-Q MDA or any filing section. The system should retrieve the relevant MDA sections on AI business and correctly state the metric is not in the filing, rather than hallucinating a figure from the financial statements.

payload.query: what is Microsoft's AI business annual revenue run rate as reported in the Q3 FY2026 10-Q

ground_truth.refusal_reason: The $37B AI ARR figure is a non-GAAP KPI disclosed on the earnings call only. The 10-Q MDA does not contain this metric — AI revenue is not broken out as a separate line item in the financial statements. A system retrieving this figure from the transcript is answering from outside the filing corpus.

notes: Analyst pushback: analyst questions about declining gaming revenue and devices — a challenge embedded in polite analyst framing. The question implies concern about the MPC segment's structural weakness. Tests whether FinBERT reads the concern or the polite register.

ground_truth.label: negative
ground_truth.rationale: Revenue down 7% in gaming and 5% in Xbox content/services are negative results. The explanatory framing ('prior year comparable that benefited from strong first-party content') is context, not mitigation — the current period results are clearly negative. FinBERT reading the financial direction should label this negative regardless of the neutral delivery register.
